note_data.title: Parallelograms and Triangles
keyPoints: A parallelogram is a quadrilateral whose opposite sides are parallel
Opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal and parallel
Opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal
Diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other
A diagonal divides a parallelogram into two congruent triangles
Triangles on the same base and between the same parallels are equal in area
A triangle and a parallelogram on the same base and between the same parallels have the ratio of areas 1:2
The median of a triangle divides it into two triangles of equal area

definitions.definition: A quadrilateral whose opposite sides are parallel is called a parallelogram
A parallelogram whose all angles are right angles is called a rectangle
A parallelogram whose all sides are equal is called a rhombus
A rectangle whose all sides are equal is called a square
A line segment joining a vertex of a triangle to the midpoint of the opposite side is called median

importantQuestions.question: Prove that opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal
importantQuestions.answer: In parallelogram ABCD, AB || DC and AD || BC. Using alternate interior angles and ASA congruence, triangles ABC and CDA are congruent. Hence AB = DC and AD = BC.

importantQuestions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other
importantQuestions.answer: Given: ABCD is a parallelogram with diagonals AC and BD intersecting at O. To Prove: AO = OC and BO = OD. Proof: In triangles AOB and COD, AB = CD (opposite sides), angle BAO = angle DCO (alternate angles), angle ABO = angle CDO (alternate angles). By ASA, triangle AOB ≅ triangle COD. Hence AO = OC and BO = OD.

commonMistakes: Students often forget to write 'Given' and 'To Prove' in geometry proofs
Confusing properties of rectangle and rhombus - remember rectangle has equal diagonals, rhombus has perpendicular diagonals
Not labeling vertices correctly in diagrams - always use ABCD for parallelograms
Writing angle measures incorrectly - opposite angles are equal, adjacent angles are supplementary
Forgetting to state which congruence rule (SAS, ASA, SSS) is being used in proofs
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 long proof question worth 5-8 marks
Always draw neat labeled diagrams - marks are awarded for correct diagrams
Memorize the standard proofs: opposite sides equal, diagonals bisect each other, triangles on same base
Practice drawing parallelogram with diagonals - this diagram appears in every BSEK paper
Learn the exact sequence of steps for each proof as given in STBB textbook
formulas: Area of triangle = ½ × base × height
Perimeter of parallelogram = 2(sum of adjacent sides)
note_data.keyDiagram: Parallelogram ABCD with diagonals AC and BD intersecting at point O. Label all vertices clearly and mark equal sides with small parallel lines. This diagram is essential for proving diagonal bisection theorem.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q4 (4 marks) — asked students to prove that diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q6 (5 marks) — required proof that triangles on the same base and between same parallels have equal areas
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q3 (3 marks) — asked to state and prove the converse of parallelogram diagonal theorem
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q5 (6 marks) — proof that median of a triangle divides it into two triangles of equal area

workedExample.question: Prove that the diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other. (5 marks)
steps: Draw parallelogram ABCD with diagonals AC and BD intersecting at point O
In triangles AOB and COD: AB || DC (opposite sides of parallelogram), so ∠BAC = ∠DCA (alternate angles)
Similarly, ∠ABD = ∠CDB (alternate angles) and AB = DC (opposite sides of parallelogram)
Therefore, triangle AOB ≅ triangle COD by ASA congruency
Hence AO = OC and BO = OD, proving diagonals bisect each other
workedExample.answer: Complete geometric proof showing triangles AOB and COD are congruent, leading to conclusion that O bisects both diagonals
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for diagram, 2 marks for identifying equal angles and sides, 1 mark for congruency statement, 1 mark for final conclusion

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by not drawing proper diagrams with correct labeling - always label all vertices clearly in geometric proofs
Avoid writing 'obviously' or 'clearly' in proofs - every step must be justified using textbook theorems and postulates exactly as stated in STBB
Never mix up 'area of triangle' with 'area of parallelogram' formulas - BSEK specifically tests the distinction between these concepts
note_data.quickRecap: Parallelograms have opposite sides equal and parallel, with diagonals bisecting each other. Triangles on same base between same parallels have equal areas. Area relationships are key - parallelogram area equals base × height, triangle area equals ½ × base × height. All proofs must use congruency theorems (SSS, SAS, ASA, RHS) as stated in textbook.
importantFormulas: Area of triangle = ½ × base × height
Area of triangle = ½ × base × corresponding altitude
